items.t: — Anglo names the Global Diamond Consortium, led by ex-De Beers chief Gareth Penny, preferred bidder for its 85%; Botswana weighs its right to match; close eyed Q4
— the $4,000 line is tested on day six of the Iran strikes and defended by the close, +1.2%; silver 56.63, platinum sheds 25.10 to 1,604.50
— the India–UK pact takes force; first duty-free gold, silver and diamond jewelry consignments ship within a day; UK scraps 96.8% of tariff lines
— the official pre-owned premium narrows to a 22.6% median over the open market; Q2 program sales $186M across 157 of 712 retailers
— watch sell-through hits 90% as H1 sales run +64%; 45% of lots beat high estimates; the million-dollar shelf already near last year's full count
— Q2 growth undershoots Beijing's own floor; Swiss exports to the mainland fell 12.1% in 2025 and a third over two years
— Rapaport: demand picking up, supply falling, prices unchanged; long cushions carry a 20–25% premium over squares; Belgium H1 exports +18%
— a car-boot find appraises at £15,000 in Oxfordshire: a 3.02ct natural diamond in 18k gold, bought as costume
— Berkshire's jewelry arm promotes Arien Gessner to chief revenue officer, Moss Makhoulian to senior vice president
— the Russian miner claims carbon-neutral certification for its operations for a second consecutive year
